The Maker

J.M. Amoedo

Custom knife maker. Each blade is forged by hand — shaped from raw steel and built to perform for a lifetime.

The Story Behind the Blades

J.M. Amoedo is a custom knife maker driven by a deep respect for the craft of blade making. Every knife that leaves the workshop is built from raw materials, shaped by hand, and tested before it goes out the door.

The work starts at the forge — raw steel drawn out, shaped under heat and hammer, then ground to geometry on the wheel. Handles are fitted from a range of natural materials: hardwoods, stabilized burls, bone, horn and brass, chosen for the knife and the person who'll carry it.

The goal with every blade is the same: a knife that feels right in the hand, holds an edge, and gets better with use.

Craft & Philosophy

Materials & Method

Steel

High-carbon and stainless steels selected for their performance characteristics — hardness, toughness, edge retention and corrosion resistance.

Handle Materials

Stabilized hardwoods, natural burls, bone, horn, antler and brass. Each handle material is chosen for how it looks, how it ages and how it feels in use.

Finishing

Each blade is hand-sanded through progressive grits, heat-treated to specification, then finished to a consistent edge before fitting and final assembly.

The Work

Recent Pieces
